title: Post-Disaster Recovery Guide
subtitle: Returning home and recovering after a disaster in India

sections.heading: Before Re-Entering Your Home
items: Wait for authorities to declare area safe
Check exterior for structural damage
Check for gas leaks - do NOT use open flame to check
Watch for snakes and scorpions
Wear sturdy shoes and gloves

sections.heading: Inside Your Home
items: Open windows to ventilate
Do NOT switch on electricity until checked by qualified electrician
Check for mold growth - wear N95 mask while cleaning
Photograph all damage for insurance/government relief claims
Dispose of food that contacted flood water
Boil water or use purification tablets before drinking

sections.heading: Health Precautions
items: Watch for waterborne diseases: cholera, typhoid, leptospirosis, hepatitis A
Use ORS for any diarrhea symptoms
Seek medical help for fever, skin infections, respiratory issues
Ensure children receive any missed vaccinations
Beware of mosquito-borne diseases - use repellent and nets

sections.heading: Government Relief & Claims
items: Register with local administration for relief
Apply for PM CARES Fund assistance (pmcares.gov.in)
Apply for state CM Relief Fund
Contact SDMA for state-level disaster relief
File insurance claims promptly with damage photographs
Beware of contractor scams

sections.heading: Mental Health
items: Disaster trauma is real - seek support
Contact iCall: +91-9152987821
Vandrevala Foundation: 1860-2662-345 (24/7)
Talk to family, friends, community members
Children may need extra support and reassurance
